title: GST Council Recommendation: Changes in GSTR-1 & GSTR-3B
summary: Recent recommendations suggest amendments to GSTR-1 and 3B for better reconciliation of ITC and sales data.
content:
The GST Council in its latest meeting has recommended several procedural changes to streamline the filing process. Key highlights include:

1. Mandatory reporting of HSN codes for all taxpayers with turnover above 5Cr.
2. Introduction of a new table in GSTR-3B for reporting reversal of ITC.
3. Synchronization of GSTR-1 and GSTR-3B deadlines to avoid late fee disparities.

Taxpayers are advised to review their ERP systems to ensure compatibility with these upcoming changes which are expected to be live from the next quarter.

title: Income Tax: New Compliance for High Value Transactions
summary: CBDT has issued new guidelines for reporting SFT (Statement of Financial Transactions) for high-value cash deposits.
content:
The Central Board of Direct Taxes (CBDT) has tightened the norms for reporting high-value transactions. 

Starting this financial year, reporting entities must include detailed PAN/Aadhaar validation for cash deposits exceeding 50 Lakhs in a single year across all accounts. This move aims to curb black money and increase the tax base. 

Professionals should ensure that their clients are well-informed about these reporting requirements to avoid scrutiny notices.

title: TDS Rate Changes for Tech Services
summary: The government has updated the TDS rates under Section 194J for technical and professional services.
content:
Section 194J has undergone minor revisions in the current finance act. 

- TDS on Fees for technical services (other than professional services) remains at 2%.
- TDS on professional services remains at 10%.
- However, a new sub-category for 'Cloud Computing Services' has been introduced with a reduced rate of 5% to promote digital infrastructure.

Ensure you update your TDS software to reflect these changes before the next deduction cycle.

title: ROC: Annual Filing Deadlines for FY 2025-26
summary: MCA has announced the calendar for annual filing (AOC-4 and MGT-7) for the previous financial year.
content:
The Ministry of Corporate Affairs (MCA) has released the filing schedule for the FY ending March 2026. 

- Form AOC-4: Within 30 days of AGM.
- Form MGT-7: Within 60 days of AGM.
- DIN KYC (DIR-3 KYC): Deadline 30th September 2026.

Early filing is encouraged to avoid last-minute server congestion on the V3 portal.

title: New GST Rules for E-commerce Operators
summary: E-commerce platforms must now implement real-time TCS (Tax Collected at Source) reporting.
content:
To increase transparency, the GST department has mandated real-time reporting of sales and TCS by e-commerce operators. 

Previously, reporting was done on a monthly basis. The new API-based integration will allow the department to track vendor sales instantly. Small vendors are expected to benefit from faster reconciliation of their ITC.

title: Tax Audit: Key Checkpoints for CAs
summary: Preparing for the tax audit season? Here are the top 5 areas of focus for Clause 44 reconciliation.
content:
Clause 44 (GST expenditure details) continues to be a major challenge for tax auditors. 

Key focus areas:
1. Matching total expenditure with P&L.
2. Correct classification of exempt vs taxable supply expenditures.
3. Identification of expenses from composition dealers.
4. Verification of RCM payments.
5. Cross-checking with GSTR-2B data.

title: MCA V3 Portal: Common Issues and Solutions
summary: Navigating the V3 portal? Here's how to resolve common DSC and Login errors.
content:
The transition to the MCA V3 portal has seen several technical hurdles. 

Common solutions:
- Clear browser cache and use Incognito mode for fresh logins.
- Ensure EmSigner is updated to the latest version.
- If 'User ID already exists' error appears, use the 'Forgot Password' link to reset the profile link.
- DSC association should be done through the 'Update DSC' utility specifically in the V3 dashboard.
